title: Read, Unread, Star, Tag, Bulk Action, and Conflict Semantics · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed operations review reconstructs read, unread, star, tag, bulk action, and conflict semantics from revision, tenant, source URL, request validators, response bytes, parsed identity, transaction, lease, state transition, output, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for consistent personal reader-state model after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evid…
objectives: Keep read, unread, starred, hidden, saved, and tag relationships scoped to principal, tenant, and entry without mutating shared content.
Make set-state commands idempotent with explicit desired state, preconditions where needed, and stable response semantics.
Define bounded all-or-nothing or per-item bulk semantics, authorization, result reconciliation, and retry behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A review Go team handles case 3443 during a multilingual university archive import. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove private reader state, desired-state commands, bulk semantics, conflicts, and count repair; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep read, unread, starred, hidden, saved, and tag relationships scoped to principal, tenant, and entry without mutating shared content.
